rag_rules: Place AGREE assumptions and guarantees in component type scopes when they refer to component ports.
Use annex agree {** ... **}; blocks for AGREE contract content.
Generate assumptions using visible feature names and concrete bounds from the requirement.
generic_rules: Component type contracts should use visible variables and ports, not implementation-only subcomponent paths.
Ensure AGREE expressions match the actual data types of AADL features.
Only include bounds or constraints explicitly stated in the requirement.
Do not assume numeric ranges unless the requirement or AADL model supports it.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in annex agree {** ... **}; blocks.
Use eq declarations for boolean range checks like eq valid_range : bool = value >= min and value <= max;
anti_patterns: Avoid placing implementation-level bindings in component type scopes.
Do not invent numeric constraints like x >= 0 unless explicitly required.
irrelevant_topics: AADL property associations (e.g., Compute_Execution_Time, Deadline) are not automatically available in AGREE scope.
Boolean data port comparisons should not be converted to integer equality unless the model supports it.
Subcomponent ports should not be referenced in component type annexes unless represented through variables.
